text.title: PMBoK 8 Focus Areas Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding PMBoK 8 Focus Areas. Each pairs a binary check and a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Rubric MUST cover all five PMBoK 8 focus areas (Initiating, Planning, Executing, Monitoring and Controlling, Closing). Skipping any focus area is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Skipping focus areas creates lifecycle blind spots; the rubric's value is comparable end-to-end coverage.

rule.statement: Each focus area MUST map to applicable performance domains; the focus area × domain matrix MUST be populated.

rationale.source: PMI PMBoK 8 — Focus Areas distribution chapter

rule.rationale: Unmapped cells indicate artefact gaps; mapping forces explicit completeness.

rule.statement: Each rubric cell MUST cite ≥1 project artefact (template, plan, register) demonstrating coverage. Empty cells forbidden.
rule.rationale: Empty cells make the rubric purely aspirational; artefact references prove the framework is real.

rule.statement: Each rubric run MUST carry a PMBoK 8 version reference and refresh date. Untracked versions forbidden.
rule.rationale: Untracked versions cause confusion when PMBoK revisions ship; audit trail makes comparison possible.

rule.statement: Cells marked NA (focus area × domain pair that doesn't apply) MUST carry a justification. Silent NA forbidden.
rule.rationale: Silent NA hides missing coverage; justification forces honest assessment.

rule.statement: If preconditions for PMBoK 8 Focus Areas fail (see Skip-If list in AGENTS.md), the operator MUST skip this methodology and document the fallback reason instead of fabricating an output.
rule.rationale: Applying PMBoK 8 Focus Areas outside its envelope produces false-precision artefacts. Explicit skip preserves epistemic discipline.
